response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with genuine low-latency and concurrent-systems experience, but his career centers on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Professional C++ exposure is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015 plus a thesis project, and there is no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or industrial-automation domain experience — the core pillars of this role. He would face a steep ramp on both the primary language and the security/OT requirements.
strengths: Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency services handling millions of requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and caching
Strong engineering-craft culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) with a track record of mentoring, workshops, and technical talks
Broad polyglot background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, indicating adaptability
concerns: Modern C++/STL depth is missing: last professional C++ role was a brief engagement ending in 2015
No industrial cybersecurity exposure — no IEC 62443, SDL, or OT protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)
No demonstrated MQTT, message-broker, or IoT platform work in the industrial-automation domain this role serves

key_factors.factor: No IEC 62443 / SDL / industrial security background
